title: Fix dark mode text contrast on widgets and sections

requirements.summary: Fix dark mode contrast so that all text, labels, values, and headings inside KPI cards, chart widgets, and dashboard sections are clearly visible when dark mode is enabled using CSS custom properties and Tailwind utility classes
acceptanceCriteria: All text inside KPI cards (title, value, description) is readable in dark mode
Chart labels, axis text, and legend text are visible in dark mode
Section headings and sub-headings on the Dashboard, Reports, and all other pages are readable in dark mode
Table cell text, column headers, and filter labels are visible in dark mode
Badge and status label text is readable in dark mode
No white-on-white or black-on-black text combinations exist anywhere in dark mode
Sidebar navigation link text and icons are clearly visible in dark mode
Header text (app title, user name, role) is clearly visible in dark mode

file_operations.description: Review and ensure all CSS custom properties for dark mode (--foreground, --card-foreground, --muted-foreground, --accent-foreground, --popover-foreground) provide high-contrast values against their respective dark backgrounds. Verify dark mode token definitions resolve to light foreground colors (e.g., OKLCH lightness >= 85%) for readability against dark backgrounds.

Ensure all text elements (title, value, description) use high-contrast Tailwind text utility classes that resolve correctly in dark mode via CSS custom properties. Replace any hardcoded color classes with theme-aware utilities (e.g., text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, text-card-foreground) to guarantee visibility in dark mode.

Update Recharts component styling to ensure all chart text (labels, axis text, legend text, tooltips) use high-contrast colors in dark mode. Apply CSS custom property-based text colors or explicit dark mode-compatible Recharts style props to CartesianGrid, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, Legend, and Bar label components.

Update Recharts component styling to ensure all chart text (labels, axis text, legend text, tooltips) use high-contrast colors in dark mode. Apply CSS custom property-based text colors or explicit dark mode-compatible Recharts style props to CartesianGrid, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, and Bar label components.

Ensure app title, user name, user role, and all interactive elements (theme toggle, logout button) use high-contrast text color utilities (text-foreground or text-card-foreground) that remain visible in dark mode. Verify icon colors also resolve correctly.

Ensure all navigation link text, icons, user profile display text, and branding footer text use high-contrast color classes (text-sidebar-foreground or text-foreground) that remain clearly visible in dark mode. Verify active/hover states also maintain readability.

Ensure badge text uses high-contrast foreground colors in dark mode for all AssetStatus and RepairOutcome variants. Use Tailwind utilities or CSS custom properties that guarantee readability against badge background colors in both light and dark modes.

Ensure all section headings, sub-headings, table headers, and text content throughout the dashboard page use high-contrast Tailwind text utilities (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) that resolve correctly in dark mode via CSS custom properties.

Ensure table headers, table cell text, filter labels, search input text, and all interactive elements use high-contrast text utilities (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) that remain visible in dark mode. Verify dropdown options and modal text also maintain readability.

Ensure table headers, table cell text, filter labels, search input text, and all interactive elements use high-contrast text utilities (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) that remain visible in dark mode. Verify dropdown options and modal text also maintain readability.

Ensure all section headings, chart titles, axis labels, legend text, table headers, and data cells use high-contrast text utilities that remain visible in dark mode. Update all Recharts components (BarChart, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, Legend) to use CSS custom property-based or explicit dark mode-compatible text colors.

Ensure table headers, table cell text, low-stock alert banner text, filter labels, and all interactive elements use high-contrast text utilities (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) that remain visible in dark mode.

Ensure table headers, table cell text, search input labels, pagination controls, and all text content use high-contrast text utilities (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) that remain visible in dark mode.

Ensure all section headings, table headers, table cell text, form labels, and modal dialog text use high-contrast text utilities (text-foreground, text-muted-foreground) that remain visible in dark mode.
